evals.name: list-and-cat-with-connection-check
evals.prompt: I just set up Draft on my machine. Can you list all my pages and show me the contents of the most recent one in markdown format?
evals.expected_output: Agent runs draft status --json, then draft start-server if needed, then draft status --json again, then draft ls --json, identifies a page, then draft cat <id> --format json
expectations: Agent starts by running draft status --json
Agent runs draft start-server if draft status indicates the server is not running or the browser is not connected
Agent lists pages with draft ls --json
Agent reads a specific page with draft cat <id> --format json

evals.name: create-and-append-with-connection-check
evals.prompt: Create a new Draft page called 'Sprint 42 Retro' and add three bullet points: what went well, what didn't, and action items. Make sure you check if you are connected first.
evals.expected_output: Agent runs draft status --json first, then draft create --json, then draft append --json with markdown bullet list via stdin
expectations: Agent starts by running draft status --json
Agent creates page with draft create 'Sprint 42 Retro' --json
Agent appends content using draft append <id> --json via stdin

evals.name: replace-section-on-recent-page
evals.prompt: In my most recent Draft page, replace the Status section with 'Status: Complete - shipped to staging on March 12th'. Check connection first and make sure you're on staging (https://markdown-editor-staging.web.app/).
evals.expected_output: Agent runs draft status --json, then draft start-server https://markdown-editor-staging.web.app/ if needed, then draft status --json again, then draft ls --json to find the most recent ID, then draft replace <id> --heading 'Status' --json
expectations: Agent starts by running draft status --json
Agent lists pages with draft ls --json to find the correct ID
Agent uses draft replace command with --json on the correct ID

evals.name: create-and-publish-roadmap
evals.prompt: I want to share my project roadmap. Create a new Draft page titled 'Project Roadmap', and then publish it to the web. Remember to check your connection first.
evals.expected_output: Agent runs draft status --json, then draft create 'Project Roadmap' --json, captures the generated ID, and runs GLOBAL_PUBLISH_PASSWORD=innosage draft publish <id> --json
expectations: Agent starts by running draft status --json
Agent creates the page and captures the ID from JSON output
Agent uses the GLOBAL_PUBLISH_PASSWORD=innosage draft publish <id> --json command with the correct ID
